title: The Last Stand
tagline: They knew they were going to die. They advanced anyway. That was the message.
hook_line: Surrounded on two sides. No retreat possible. 1,000 men against 2.5 million. They chose to attack.

scenes.title: Surrounded
scenes.narration: Surrounded. Immortals behind. Persian army in front. No retreat possible. Against any military logic, the Spartans chose to advance.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting aerial view, the 1000 remaining defenders — 300 Spartans and 700 Thespians — surrounded on both sides, Persian forces ahead in the pass and Immortals descending the mountain behind, the impossibility of their position geometrically clear, and yet the formation turning to face the frontal assault, choosing attack over defense, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Advance
scenes.narration: They pushed forward out of the narrowest part of the pass — where the geography had protected them — into the wider section. They gave up their defensive advantage deliberately. To kill as many Persians as possible.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, the Spartan phalanx advancing forward out of the narrow Hot Gates into the wider area beyond, their defensive geometry sacrificed deliberately, the decision to trade the position's advantage for maximum offensive impact visible in the forward motion, Persian forces ahead, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Final Battle
scenes.narration: They fought with spears until the spears broke. They fought with swords until the swords broke. They fought with their hands and their teeth. Herodotus recorded each weapon as it was spent.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, the final battle in progression — Spartans with long spears, then shorter swords, then bare-handed combat, the stages of escalation visible in one compressed cinematic image, the fury and totality of the last stand, Persian dead everywhere, the 300 and the Thespians fighting with everything remaining to them, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: Leonidas Falls
scenes.narration: Leonidas was killed in the fighting. The Spartans fought to recover his body. Herodotus records that they drove the Persians back four times to retrieve their king.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, the recovery of Leonidas's body — Spartan warriors fighting through Persian forces in a desperate protective action, not retreating but advancing to bring their king back, four times pushing the Persians back from the body, the loyalty visible as a physical force, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Arrows
scenes.narration: Finally, with the Immortals closing from behind, the remaining Spartans and Thespians withdrew to a small hill. Xerxes ordered his archers to finish it. Herodotus says their arrows darkened the sun.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, the final formation on the small hill — the last Spartans and Thespians in tight formation on elevated ground, the sky above them darkening with Persian arrows to the point where the sun is blocked, the famous image of arrow-darkened sky above the last defenders, dramatic and epic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: A Spartan's Answer
scenes.narration: 'Good,' a Spartan reportedly said when told the arrows would block the sun. 'Then we shall fight in the shade.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, close-up of a Spartan warrior on the hill looking up at the arrow-darkened sky, a slight smile at the corner of his mouth, the famous line visible in his expression before he speaks it, crimson cloak, battered armor, spear still in hand, utterly unbroken, the shade of the arrows falling on him, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Last Stand
scenes.narration: The arrows fell. The last defenders fell. The battle of Thermopylae was over. It had taken Xerxes — with the largest army in history — three days and a betrayer to move 1,000 men.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, the end of the battle — arrows falling like rain on the small hill, the last Spartan and Thespian defenders overwhelmed by the sheer volume of missiles, the silence of battle's end beginning, the hill covered in fallen defenders surrounded by Persian forces, the enormity and finality of the moment, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: Xerxes's Rage
scenes.narration: Xerxes desecrated Leonidas's body — ordered his head cut off and his body crucified. This was his only known act of deliberate cruelty in the entire campaign. The Spartans had made him angry.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, Xerxes giving the order for desecration — his expression showing the anger that the Spartans had provoked in the only man who never showed anger, generals receiving the unusual order with surprise, the act of a king who had never needed to do this to any enemy before, the Spartans had gotten under his skin, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: What They Bought
scenes.narration: The three days at Thermopylae bought Greece time to prepare. The Athenian fleet evacuated the population of Athens. The Greek alliance solidified. The delay cost Xerxes the season.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, the ripple effects of three days — Athenian ships evacuating civilian population, Greek city-states sending messengers to coordinate, the Athenian navy preparing at Salamis, the time bought at Thermopylae being spent in decisive preparation, the dead Spartans' gift visible in the activity of the living Greeks, dramatic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Inscription
scenes.narration: At the site of the battle, a stone was placed. The inscription read: 'Go tell the Spartans, stranger passing by, that here, obedient to their laws, we lie.'
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic oil painting, the stone monument at Thermopylae in dramatic lighting — the inscription visible in ancient Greek letters, the pass behind it, the mountains beyond, the sea in the distance, the place where the world was briefly held, the silence of the aftermath, the permanence of what was done there, cinematic and final, 1080x1920 vertical

viral_hook: 'Then we shall fight in the shade.' The Spartans' response when told the arrows would block the sun.
highlight_note: The Spartan's response to arrow-darkened sky — lightly spoken, utterly unbroken — is the most famous moment in Thermopylae history and the emotional peak of the entire season.
short_title: We Shall Fight in the Shade
rewatch_bait: Xerxes ordering the desecration of Leonidas's body — his only known act of deliberate cruelty in the campaign. The Spartans made the most powerful man in the world angry. That means something.
youtube_title: 'The Arrows Will Block the Sun.' 'Then We Shall Fight in the Shade.' | Gods & Glory
lesson: The 300 didn't win. They knew they wouldn't. They measured victory differently — in time bought, in courage shown, in what it means to die obedient to your laws.
arc_notes: EP008 closes the Thermopylae arc. The inscription ends it perfectly — not triumphant, not tragic, just true. Sets up EP009 (burning of Athens) and EP010 (legacy) with the emotional residue of the last stand still present.
